raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 1.0,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The request 'Improve this draft' is broad. Should the focus be on (A) sentence-level polish (grammar, flow, conciseness) or (B) higher-level scientific argumentation (structure, contribution framing, evidence-claim alignment)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request to 'improve' the draft may include reframing the core contribution for greater impact.",
    "R1.2": "Improving a research draft centrally involves verifying the logical flow of the argument from premises to conclusions.",
    "R1.3": "A key aspect of improvement is ensuring that all claims made in the text are properly supported by the reported results.",
    "R1.4": "The draft's quality can be improved by assessing the novelty claim against the cited prior work.",
    "R1.5": "The current text mixes results and related work, so improving the narrative arc through structural reorganization is a load-bearing task.",
    "R1.6": "Improving a draft is a load-bearing responsibility that includes polishing grammar, style, and flow at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "R1.7": "A comprehensive improvement includes checking the accuracy and coverage of citations to ensure robust scholarship.",
    "RX.1": "The agent must disclose any uncertainty in its assessment of the draft's scientific merit or clarity.",
    "RX.2": "When improving the text, the agent must scrupulously avoid exaggerating claims or the significance of the results.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must adhere to the scope of 'improving the draft' and not conduct new research or analysis.",
    "RX.4": "The agent must ensure its edits do not introduce misleading information or other potential downstream harms.",
    "RX.5": "All suggested improvements must be traceable to specific aspects of the original text and the improvement goals."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The request 'Improve this draft' is broad. Should the focus be on (A) sentence-level polish (grammar, flow, conciseness) or (B) higher-level scientific argumentation (structure, contribution framing, evidence-claim alignment)?

rationale.R1.1: The request to 'improve' the draft may include reframing the core contribution for greater impact.
rationale.R1.2: Improving a research draft centrally involves verifying the logical flow of the argument from premises to conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: A key aspect of improvement is ensuring that all claims made in the text are properly supported by the reported results.
rationale.R1.4: The draft's quality can be improved by assessing the novelty claim against the cited prior work.
rationale.R1.5: The current text mixes results and related work, so improving the narrative arc through structural reorganization is a load-bearing task.
rationale.R1.6: Improving a draft is a load-bearing responsibility that includes polishing grammar, style, and flow at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.R1.7: A comprehensive improvement includes checking the accuracy and coverage of citations to ensure robust scholarship.
rationale.RX.1: The agent must disclose any uncertainty in its assessment of the draft's scientific merit or clarity.
rationale.RX.2: When improving the text, the agent must scrupulously avoid exaggerating claims or the significance of the results.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must adhere to the scope of 'improving the draft' and not conduct new research or analysis.
rationale.RX.4: The agent must ensure its edits do not introduce misleading information or other potential downstream harms.
rationale.RX.5: All suggested improvements must be traceable to specific aspects of the original text and the improvement goals.
